children.heading: Privacy Act of 1974
children.label: a Privacy Act of 1974
children.text: Nothing in the Compact shall affect the obligations and responsibilities of the FBI under (commonly known as the “Privacy Act of 1974”).section 552a of title 5

children.heading: Access to certain records not affected
children.label: b Access to certain records not affected
B
children.text: the National Child Protection Act [ et seq.];134 U.S.C. 40101See References in Text note below.1

children.label: C
children.text: the Brady Handgun Violence Prevention Act (; );Public Law 103–159107 Stat. 1536

children.label: D
children.text: the Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994 (; ) or any amendment made by that Act;Public Law 103–322108 Stat. 2074

children.label: E
children.text: the United States Housing Act of 1937 ( et seq.); or42 U.S.C. 1437

children.label: F
children.text: the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 ( et seq.); or25 U.S.C. 4101
any direct access to Federal criminal history records authorized by law.

children.heading: Authority of FBI under Departments of State, Justice, and Commerce, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriation Act, 1973
children.label: c Authority of FBI under Departments of State, Justice, and Commerce, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriation Act, 1973
children.text: Nothing in the Compact shall be construed to affect the authority of the FBI under the Departments of State, Justice, and Commerce, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriation Act, 1973 ( ()).Public Law 92–54486 Stat. 1115

children.heading: Chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: d Chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: The Council shall not be considered to be a Federal advisory committee for purposes of chapter 10 of title 5.

children.heading: Members of Council not Federal officers or employees
children.label: e Members of Council not Federal officers or employees

children.text: to be, for any purpose other than to effect the Compact, officers or employees of the United States (as defined in sections 2104 and 2105 of title 5); or
to become entitled by reason of Council membership to any compensation or benefit payable or made available by the Federal Government to its officers or employees.
